water_temp_read.read: Water temperature is in a favorable trout range.

light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.
fly_reasoning.zebra_midge: Small and natural for clear, pressured, technical conditions.
fly_reasoning.pheasant_tail: A reliable natural nymph when trout are feeding but cautious.

generated_text.quick_read: Trout fishing conditions on the Chattahoochee River are very good with ideal water temperatures and recent stocking supporting productive patterns. Expect trout to be actively feeding near riffles, runs, seams, and structure.
generated_text.best_window: The best window is day 2 morning due to cooler temperatures around 76°F, light wind at 2.3 mph, minimal sky cover, and very low rain probability, creating ideal conditions for trout activity and effective fishing.
generated_text.current_conditions: Current USGS conditions report water temperature at 55.6°F, flow at 991 cfs, gage height of 2.18 ft, and turbidity at 6.4 NTU.
generated_text.weather_setup: Current weather features 19% sky cover, pressure at 30.05 inHg with a neutral trend, west wind averaging 5.6 mph with gusts up to 17.3 mph, and a 27% chance of light rain totaling 0.02 inches.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will favor comfortable feeding lanes near riffles, runs, seams, and structure, especially where cover is available. Presentations should focus on clean drifts with smaller natural nymphs, midges, soft hackles, opportunistic streamers, and stocked-trout attractors like egg patterns and squirmy worms to capitalize on recent stocking.
